description.english: Scan your first item.
Survive your first hour on the job.
Restock 10 items onto shelves.
Clean your first mess.
Accept your first delivery.
Organize 5 boxes in the backroom.
Fix your first blackout.
Fix 3 blackouts without mistakes.
Install an alarm system.
Expand the store.
Serve 10 customers without making a mistake.
Serve 20 customers in a single night.
Sell 5 kebabs.
Every shelf unlocked. Every slot filled. No empty space left behind.
Earn £2 000 total.
Earn £10 000 total.
Take your medication for the first time.
Reach critical stress and recover.
Check the cameras 10 times in one night.
You found what was buried.
Let viewers trigger 10 actions in one night.
Survive a night with 25 viewer triggers.
Say it out loud.
A plan. A promise.
Justice doesn’t live here anymore.
The paperwork finally caught up to you.
You came back. That counts for something.
The therapy continues. The nightmares don’t.
You talked to Sarah… or you think you did.
You chose fire.
It ended before you could stop it.
You reconnected… but there’s still work to do.
You reunited with your family and got your parental rights back.
Earn $5000 total. The bills pile up, but the nights feel heavier.
Survive 5 days. The night shift still feels unfamiliar.
Survive 15 days. The store begins to feel like a second home.
Survive a full month. The nights have become your routine.
Serve 25 customers. The bell interrupts the silence again and again.
Serve 150 customers. The counter never seems to stay empty.
Serve 250 customers. Tonight the line never truly disappears.
Sell 100 items. The scanner finally finds a steady rhythm.
Sell 500 items. The constant beeping fills the empty store.
Sell 1500 items. Your hands move before your mind even notices.
Place 50 items on shelves. The shelves never stay full for long.
Place 250 items on shelves. The backroom door swings open all night.
Place 1000 items on shelves. You know every shelf in this place by heart.
Upgrade the shop to 25%. The store begins to look less forgotten.
Upgrade the shop to 50%. The shop slowly changes under the buzzing lights.
Fully upgrade the shop. For a moment, the place almost feels respectable.
Reach 100 Strength. The stockroom builds strength faster than any gym.
Reach 100 Charisma. Even the strangest customers seem to listen.
Reach 100 Intelligence. You start noticing things others would miss.
Reach 100 Resistance. Long nights harden more than just your routine.
Reach 50 Karma. The regulars start to trust the man behind the counter.
Reach 150 Karma. Your reputation spreads among late-night visitors.
Reach 250 Karma. The whole neighborhood knows your shop.
Reach -100 Karma. Some nights leave stains that never wash off.
Upgrade the apartment to 25%. The apartment begins to feel less empty.
Upgrade the apartment to 50%. Slowly, the place begins to resemble a home.
Fully upgrade the apartment. Even nightmares need a comfortable bed.
Spend $25 on gambling. Just one more try before the shift.
Spend $100 on gambling. The thrill keeps pulling you back.
Spend $500 on gambling. You keep playing even when the lights feel wrong.
Prepare 5 kebabs or sandwiches. The smell fills the quiet shop.
Prepare 15 kebabs or sandwiches. The small kitchen never stays quiet.
Prepare 30 kebabs or sandwiches. Even cooking feels different after midnight.
Fill every storage cabinet in the stockroom. There’s barely space left to move.
Clean 25 messes. Every stain tells a small story.
Clean 100 messes. The floor shines under the buzzing lights.
Clean 250 messes. The store looks perfect… at least for a moment.
Spend $25 on medicine. Some nights require a little help.
Spend $100 on medicine. The pills help quiet the thoughts.
Spend $250 on medicine. Sometimes the silence needs assistance.
Make 5 coffees. The smell of coffee fights the late hours.
Make 15 coffees. Caffeine keeps the night moving.
Make 30 coffees. Without coffee, the night would never end.
Complete all apartment interactions before starting a shift. Preparation makes the darkness easier to face.
Finish the game completing the entire checklist. Nothing escaped your attention.
The good boy. Even the darkest nights feel lighter when you remember him.
